abstract: Training semantic parsers for large-scale knowledge bases like Freebase typically requires expensive annotations of logical forms. In this paper, we present a method for learning a semantic parser using only question-answer pairs, making it more scalable to open-domain data. To address the challenge of navigating the vast space of logical predicates, we introduce two key strategies: first, we establish a coarse alignment between natural language phrases and predicates by leveraging a large text corpus and the knowledge base itself; second, we introduce a "bridging" operation that infers implicit relations by generating predicates compatible with adjacent logical components. We evaluate our approach on a standard benchmark, where our system achieves 62% accuracy, outperforming the previous state-of-the-art despite using weaker supervision. Furthermore, we introduce WEBQUESTIONS, a new dataset of questions collected via search engine queries and answered by crowdsourced workers, demonstrating that our parser effectively handles more diverse and realistic natural language challenges.
